text.title: Handover Pack Template Outsource Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Handover Pack Template Outsource methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every pack MUST name a single receiving owner (full name + contact) on the cover page; generic groups ('the team', 'whoever picks up') are forbidden.
rule.rationale: orphaned packs are read by nobody and the work doubles.

rule.statement: Every decision in the decision-log section MUST cite source (PR url, ADR id, transcript timestamp, ticket id); decisions without anchor are rejected.
rule.rationale: specialists summarising from memory invent rationale; anchored citations make it falsifiable.

rule.statement: Every open-issues row MUST carry owner_after_handover and expected_by_date; rows with unknown owner or no date are rejected.
rule.rationale: open issues without owner+date stall the receiver for weeks.

rule.statement: Every runbook step listed in the pack MUST have been executed end-to-end by the outgoing specialist within 30 days of pack publication; un-tested steps are flagged 'requires-receiver-validation'.
rule.rationale: stale runbooks are worse than no runbook — the receiver follows them and breaks prod.

rule.statement: Pack MUST list an explicit acceptance gate: receiver acknowledges in writing, lists 3 questions or 'none', and dates the acknowledgement.
rule.rationale: without an explicit gate, ambiguity about 'is the handover done' lingers for months.

rule.statement: Every contact in the contacts section MUST have been contacted (any channel) within the last 60 days; stale contacts (>60d) MUST be flagged 'verify-before-use'.
rule.rationale: people change roles; stale contact lists waste the receiver's first week.
